question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type: application/protobuf containing a protobuf message with a string field set to a very long string (e.g., 100MB), causing mergeFrom() to allocate a large String object in memory and exhaust heap space?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Content-Type: application/protobuf request with a body that triggers a protobuf parsing exception in mergeFrom() that is not caught by the custom converter, causing an unhandled exception to propagate and potentially expose internal error details in the HTTP response?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type: application/protobuf and a body containing a protobuf message with a bytes field set to a very large byte array, causing mergeFrom() to allocate a large byte[] on the heap and trigger an OutOfMemoryError?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter's response buffering behavior to cause the server to hold large response bodies in memory simultaneously across many concurrent requests, amplifying memory pressure and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: initialize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a query parameter containing a Unicode variant of an enum value (e.g., using Unicode lookalike characters) that the case-insensitive converter registered by ApplicationConversionService.addApplicationConverters() normalizes to a valid enum, bypassing input sanitization?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Content-Type: application/protobuf request with a body containing a protobuf message that has a map field with a very large number of entries, causing mergeFrom() to allocate a large HashMap and exhaust heap memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type: application/protobuf and a body that is valid protobuf encoding but represents a message with field numbers in the range reserved for extensions, causing mergeFrom() with the empty ExtensionRegistry to store them as unknown fields and potentially bypass field-level validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with multiple If-None-Match header values (comma-separated ETags) to the /api/* endpoints to cause the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter to perform multiple ETag comparisons, consuming extra CPU and enabling a low-cost denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Content-Type: application/protobuf request with a body containing a protobuf message with a oneof field set to multiple alternatives simultaneously (which is invalid but may be accepted by mergeFrom()), causing the last-wins behavior to silently override the first-parsed value and bypass validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type: application/protobuf;charset=UTF-8 (with parameters) to test whether protobufMediaType.isCompatibleWith(contentType) still matches, triggering the mergeFrom() path with a content type that includes unexpected parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Content-Type: application/protobuf request with a body containing a protobuf message with a string field containing null bytes or control characters, causing mergeFrom() to accept the string and pass it to downstream processing that does not expect such characters, leading to injection or parsing errors?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with If-None-Match: * (wildcard ETag) to an /api/* endpoint to test whether the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter handles the wildcard correctly, potentially causing all responses to be treated as not-modified and returning empty 304 responses?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type: APPLICATION/PROTOBUF (uppercase) to test whether the MediaType comparison in protobufMediaType.isCompatibleWith(contentType) is case-sensitive, potentially bypassing the protobuf parsing path or triggering it unexpectedly?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Content-Type: application/protobuf request with a body containing a protobuf message with a sint64 field set to Long.MIN_VALUE, causing arithmetic operations on the zigzag-decoded value to overflow and produce unexpected results in downstream processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type: application/protobuf and a body containing a protobuf message with a fixed64 field set to 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF, causing downstream code that interprets the field as a signed long to receive Long.MIN_VALUE and trigger unexpected behavior?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to an /api/* endpoint with a very large Accept header to cause the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter to include it in ETag computation or response buffering, consuming excessive memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Content-Type: application/protobuf request with a body containing a protobuf message with a group type (deprecated wire type 3/4), causing mergeFrom() to enter a recursive parsing loop and exhaust the call stack?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: initialize()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a query parameter value that is a valid enum name in a different locale (e.g., Turkish locale where 'i'.toUpperCase() = 'İ') to exploit locale-dependent case conversion in the ApplicationConversionService converter, causing unexpected enum resolution?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: readInternal()] Can an unprivileged external user send a Content-Type: application/protobuf request with a body containing a protobuf message with a varint field encoded using the maximum number of bytes (10 bytes for a 64-bit varint), causing mergeFrom() to accept the overlong encoding and potentially bypass length-based input validation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to an /api/* endpoint with a Transfer-Encoding: chunked body to test whether the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter correctly buffers chunked responses for ETag computation, potentially causing incorrect ETag values or response corruption?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: protobufHttpMessageConverter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with Content-Type: application/protobuf and a body containing a protobuf message with a field number of 0 (invalid in protobuf), causing mergeFrom() to throw an InvalidProtocolBufferException that is not properly handled, leaking internal error details?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/config/RestJavaConfiguration.java -> Scope: '] [Function: etagFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request to an /api/* endpoint with a crafted Range header to test whether the ShallowEtagHeaderFilter interacts incorrectly with partial content responses, causing incorrect ETag computation or response body corruption?
